records.chapter: Chapter on the husband's Rights Over His Wife
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4516 - Muhammad ibn al-Fudhayl narrated from Shurayh al-Wabishi, from Jabir, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has ordained jihad upon men and jihad upon women. The jihad of a man is to spend his wealth and sacrifice his blood until he is killed in the way of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic. And the jihad of a woman is to be patient with what she faces from the harm and jealousy of her husband."
records.arabicText: 4516 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ شُرَيْسٍ اَلْوَابِشِيِّ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ كَتَبَ عَلَى اَلرِّجَالِ اَلْجِهَادَ وَ عَلَى اَلنِّسَاءِ اَلْجِهَادَ فَجِهَادُ اَلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَبْذُلَ مَالَهُ وَ دَمَهُ حَتَّى يُقْتَلَ فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ جِهَادُ اَلْمَرْأَةِ أَنْ تَصْبِرَ عَلَى مَا تَرَى مِنْ أَذَى زَوْجِهَا وَ غَيْرَتِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4516 - Muhammad ibn al-Fudhayl narrated from Shurayh al-Wabishi, from Jabir, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has ordained jihad upon men and jihad upon women. The jihad of a man is to spend his wealth and sacrifice his blood until he is killed in the way of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic. And the jihad of a woman is to be patient with what she faces from the harm and jealousy of her husband."

records.chapter: Chapter on the husband's Rights Over His Wife
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4517 - Imam (as) said: "Indeed, those who are saved from among the men are few, and from among the women, even fewer and fewer."
records.arabicText: 4517 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: إِنَّ "اَلنَّاجِيَ مِنَ اَلرِّجَالِ قَلِيلٌ وَ مِنَ اَلنِّسَاءِ أَقَلُّ وَ أَقَلُّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4517 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, those who are saved from among the men are few, and from among the women, even fewer and fewer."

records.chapter: Chapter on the husband's Rights Over His Wife
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4518 - And in another narration, Imam (as) said:
"The jihad of a woman is being a good wife."
records.arabicText: 4518 - وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ قَالَ: "جِهَادُ اَلْمَرْأَةِ حُسْنُ اَلتَّبَعُّلِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4518 - And in another narration, Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The jihad of a woman is being a good wife."

records.chapter: Chapter on the husband's Rights Over His Wife
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4519 - Muhammad ibn al-Fudhayl narrated from Sa'd ibn Umar al-Jallab, who said: Abu Abdillah (as) said:
"Any woman who spends the night while her husband is angry with her over a rightful matter, her prayer will not be accepted until he is pleased with her."
records.arabicText: 4519 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عُمَرَ اَلْجَلاَّبِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أَيُّمَا اِمْرَأَةٍ بَاتَتْ وَ زَوْجُهَا عَلَيْهَا سَاخِطٌ فِي حَقٍّ لَمْ تُقْبَلْ مِنْهَا صَلاَةٌ حَتَّى يَرْضَى عَنْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4519 - Muhammad ibn al-Fudhayl narrated from Sa'd ibn Umar al-Jallab, who said: Abu Abdillah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Any woman who spends the night while her husband is angry with her over a rightful matter, her prayer will not be accepted until he is pleased with her."

records.chapter: Chapter on the husband's Rights Over His Wife
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4520 - Al-Sakuni narrated from Jafar ibn Muhammad, from his father (as), who said:
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
"Any woman who leaves her house without her husband's permission has no right to maintenance until she returns."
records.arabicText: 4520 - وَ رَوَى اَلسَّكُونِيُّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "أَيُّمَا اِمْرَأَةٍ خَرَجَتْ مِنْ بَيْتِهَا بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِ زَوْجِهَا فَلاَ نَفَقَةَ لَهَا حَتَّى تَرْجِعَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4520 - Al-Sakuni narrated from Jafar ibn Muhammad, from his father (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
"Any woman who leaves her house without her husband's permission has no right to maintenance until she returns."

records.chapter: Chapter on the husband's Rights Over His Wife
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4521 - Imam (as) said:
"Any woman who applies perfume for someone other than her husband, her prayer will not be accepted until she washes off the perfume as she would wash after a state of major ritual impurity (janabah)."
records.arabicText: 4521 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: "أَيُّمَا اِمْرَأَةٍ تَطَيَّبَتْ لِغَيْرِ زَوْجِهَا لَمْ تُقْبَلْ مِنْهَا صَلاَةٌ حَتَّى تَغْتَسِلَ مِنْ طِيبِهَا كَغُسْلِهَا مِنْ جَنَابَتِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4521 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Any woman who applies perfume for someone other than her husband, her prayer will not be accepted until she washes off the perfume as she would wash after a state of major ritual impurity (janabah)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the husband's Rights Over His Wife
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4522 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"It is not appropriate for a woman to perfume her clothes with incense when she leaves her house."
records.arabicText: 4522 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ يَنْبَغِي لِلْمَرْأَةِ أَنْ تُجَمِّرَ ثَوْبَهَا إِذَا خَرَجَتْ مِنْ بَيْتِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4522 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is not appropriate for a woman to perfume her clothes with incense when she leaves her house."

records.chapter: Chapter on the husband's Rights Over His Wife
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4523 - Imam (as) said:
"Any woman who removes her clothing outside her husband's house or without his permission remains under the curse of Allah (swt) until she returns to her home."
records.arabicText: 4523 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: "أَيُّمَا اِمْرَأَةٍ وَضَعَتْ ثَوْبَهَا فِي غَيْرِ مَنْزِلِ زَوْجِهَا أَوْ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِهِ لَمْ تَزَلْ فِي لَعْنَةِ اَللَّهِ إِلَى أَنْ تَرْجِعَ إِلَى بَيْتِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4523 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Any woman who removes her clothing outside her husband's house or without his permission remains under the curse of Allah (swt) until she returns to her home."

records.chapter: Chapter on the husband's Rights Over His Wife
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4524 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) that he said:
"Any woman who says to her husband, 'I have never seen any good from you,' then her deeds are rendered void."
records.arabicText: 4524 - وَ رَوَى جَمِيلُ بْنُ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "أَيُّمَا اِمْرَأَةٍ قَالَتْ لِزَوْجِهَا مَا رَأَيْتُ قَطُّ مِنْ وَجْهِكَ خَيْراً فَقَدْ حَبِطَ عَمَلُهَا ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4524 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) that he said:
"Any woman who says to her husband,
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'I have never seen any good from you,' then her deeds are rendered void."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4525 - Al-Alaa ibn Razin narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that the Messenger of Allah (swt) said:
"Archangel Jibril (as) advised me regarding women so much that I thought divorce should not be permissible except in the case of clear indecency."
records.arabicText: 4525 - رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ بْنُ رَزِينٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "أَوْصَانِي جَبْرَئِيلُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِالْمَرْأَةِ حَتَّى ظَنَنْتُ أَنَّهُ لاَ يَنْبَغِي طَلاَقُهَا إِلاَّ مِنْ فَاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4525 - Al-Alaa ibn Razin narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that the Messenger of Allah (swt) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Archangel Jibril (as) advised me regarding women so much that I thought divorce should not be permissible except in the case of clear indecency."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4526 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdillah (as) about the right of a woman over her husband.
Imam (as) said: "He should satisfy her hunger, clothe her body, and if she acts ignorantly, he should forgive her."
records.arabicText: 4526 - وَ سَأَلَ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ حَقِّ اَلْمَرْأَةِ عَلَى زَوْجِهَا قَالَ "يُشْبِعُ بَطْنَهَا وَ يَكْسُو جُثَّتَهَا وَ إِنْ جَهِلَتْ غَفَرَ لَهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4526 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdillah (as) about the right of a woman over her husband.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He should satisfy her hunger, clothe her body, and if she acts ignorantly, he should forgive her."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4527 - Ibrahim (as), the Friend of the Most Merciful (swt), complained to Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, about the behavior of Sarah {s.a}. So Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to him:
"The example of a woman is like the example of a rib, if you try to straighten it, it will break, and if you leave it as it is, you will benefit from it."
I asked: "Who said this?"
Imam (as) became angry and then said: "By Allah (swt), this is the saying of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family)."
records.arabicText: 4527 - : "إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلَ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ شَكَا إِلَى اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ خُلُقَ سَارَةَ فَأَوْحَى اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَيْهِ أَنَّ مَثَلَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ مَثَلُ اَلضِّلْعِ إِنْ أَقَمْتَهُ اِنْكَسَرَ وَ إِنْ تَرَكْتَهُ اِسْتَمْتَعْتَ بِهِ " قُلْتُ مَنْ قَالَ هَذَا فَغَضِبَ ثُمَّ قَالَ "هَذَا وَ اَللَّهِ قَوْلُ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4527 - Ibrahim (as), the Friend of the Most Merciful (swt), complained to Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, about the behavior of Sarah {s.a}. So Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to him:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The example of a woman is like the example of a rib, if you try to straighten it, it will break, and if you leave it as it is, you will benefit from it."
I asked: "Who said this?"
Imam (as) became angry and then said: "By Allah (swt), this is the saying of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4528 - Abu Abdillah (as) said:
"My father (as) had a wife who used to harm him, yet he would forgive her."
records.arabicText: 4528 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كَانَتْ لِأَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اِمْرَأَةٌ وَ كَانَتْ تُؤْذِيهِ فَكَانَ يَغْفِرُ لَهَا ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4528 - Abu Abdillah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "My father (as) had a wife who used to harm him, yet he would forgive her."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4529 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I heard Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) say:
"Whoever has a wife and does not provide her with clothing to cover her modesty and food to sustain her strength, it is the duty of the Imam to separate them."
records.arabicText:
4529 - وَ رَوَى عَاصِمُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ:
"مَنْ كَانَتْ عِنْدَهُ اِمْرَأَةٌ فَلَمْ يَكْسُهَا مَا يُوَارِي عَوْرَتَهَا وَ يُطْعِمْهَا مَا يُقِيمُ صُلْبَهَا كَانَ حَقّاً عَلَى اَلْإِمَامِ أَنْ يُفَرِّقَ بَيْنَهُمَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4529 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I heard Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever has a wife and does not provide her with clothing to cover her modesty and food to sustain her strength, it is the duty of the Imam to separate them."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4530 - Rubi ibn Abdullah and Al-Fudhayl ibn Yasar narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic:
"And whoever has his provision restricted, let him spend from what Allah (swt) has given him" (Surah At-Talaq 65:7).
Imam (as) said: "If he provides for her what sustains her strength along with clothing, then it is sufficient. Otherwise, they shall be separated."
records.arabicText:
4530 - وَ رَوَى رِبْعِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ وَ اَلْفُضَيْلُ بْنُ يَسَارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ:
۝ وَ مَنْ قُدِرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقُهُ فَلْيُنْفِقْ مِمّٰا آتٰاهُ اَللّٰهُ ۝
قَالَ "إِنْ أَنْفَقَ عَلَيْهَا مَا يُقِيمُ ظَهْرَهَا مَعَ كِسْوَةٍ وَ إِلاَّ فُرِّقَ بَيْنَهُمَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4530 - Rubi ibn Abdullah and Al-Fudhayl ibn Yasar narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic:
"And whoever has his provision restricted, let him spend from what Allah (swt) has given him" (Surah At-Talaq 65:7).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he provides for her what sustains her strength along with clothing, then it is sufficient. Otherwise, they shall be separated."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4531 - Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) who said:
"If a woman performs her five daily prayers, fasts during her month (Ramadan), performs Hajj to the House of her Lord (azj), obeys her husband, and acknowledges the right of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), then she may enter Paradise through whichever gate she wishes."
records.arabicText:
4531 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو اَلصَّبَّاحِ اَلْكِنَانِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ:
"إِذَا صَلَّتِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ خَمْسَهَا وَ صَامَتْ شَهْرَهَا وَ حَجَّتْ بَيْتَ رَبِّهَا وَ أَطَاعَتْ زَوْجَهَا وَ عَرَفَتْ حَقَّ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَلْتَدْخُلْ مِنْ أَيِّ أَبْوَابِ اَلْجِنَانِ شَاءَتْ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4531 - Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a woman performs her five daily prayers, fasts during her month (Ramadan), performs Hajj to the House of her Lord (azj), obeys her husband, and acknowledges the right of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), then she may enter Paradise through whichever gate she wishes."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4532 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
A man from the Ansar, during the time of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family), went out for some errands and instructed his wife not to leave her house until he returned.
Her father became ill, so the woman sent a message to the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) saying: "My husband has gone out and instructed me not to leave my house until he returns, but my father is ill. Do you command me to visit him?"
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "No, stay in your house and obey your husband."
Then her father died, so she sent another message, saying: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), my father has died. Do you command me to perform the funeral prayer for him?"
He (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "No, stay in your house and obey your husband."
After the man was buried, the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) sent her a message saying: "Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has forgiven you and your father because of your obedience to your husband."
records.arabicText: 4532 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "إِنَّ رَجُلاً مِنَ اَلْأَنْصَارِ عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ خَرَجَ فِي بَعْضِ حَوَائِجِهِ وَ عَهِدَ إِلَى اِمْرَأَتِهِ عَهْداً أَلاَّ تَخْرُجَ مِنْ بَيْتِهَا حَتَّى يَقْدَمَ" قَالَ "وَ إِنَّ أَبَاهَا مَرِضَ فَبَعَثَتِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ إِلَى رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَقَالَتْ إِنَّ زَوْجِي خَرَجَ وَ عَهِدَ إِلَيَّ أَنْ لاَ أَخْرُجَ مِنْ بَيْتِي حَتَّى يَقْدَمَ وَ إِنَّ أَبِي مَرِيضٌ فَتَأْمُرُنِي أَنْ أَعُودَهُ فَقَالَ "لاَ اِجْلِسِي فِي بَيْتِكِ وَ أَطِيعِي زَوْجَكِ" " قَالَ "فَمَاتَ فَبَعَثَتْ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَتْ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ إِنَّ أَبِي قَدْ مَاتَ فَتَأْمُرُنِي أَنْ أُصَلِّيَ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالَ "لاَ اِجْلِسِي فِي بَيْتِكِ وَ أَطِيعِي زَوْجَكِ" " قَالَ "فَدُفِنَ اَلرَّجُلُ فَبَعَثَ إِلَيْهَا، رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ "أَنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ قَدْ غَفَرَ لَكِ وَ لِأَبِيكِ بِطَاعَتِكِ لِزَوْجِكِ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4532 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
A man from the Ansar, during the time of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family), went out for some errands and instructed his wife not to leave her house until he returned.
Her father became ill, so the woman sent a message to the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) saying: "My husband has gone out and instructed me not to leave my house until he returns, but my father is ill. Do you command me to visit him?"
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "No, stay in your house and obey your husband."
Then her father died, so she sent another message, saying: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), my father has died. Do you command me to perform the funeral prayer for him?"
He (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "No, stay in your house and obey your husband."
After the man was buried, the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) sent her a message saying: "Indeed, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has forgiven you and your father because of your obedience to your husband."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4533 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the saying of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic:
"Protect yourselves and your families from a Fire" (Surah At-Tahrim, 66:6).
How do we protect them?
Imam (as) said: "You command them and forbid them."
It was said to him: "We command them and forbid them, but they do not accept."
Imam (as) said: "When you have commanded them and forbidden them, then you have fulfilled your responsibility."
records.arabicText:
4533 - : وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: ۝ قُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ وَ أَهْلِيكُمْ نٰاراً ۝
كَيْفَ نَقِيهِنَّ قَالَ "تَأْمُرُونَهُنَّ وَ تَنْهَوْنَهُنَّ" قِيلَ لَهُ إِنَّا نَأْمُرُهُنَّ وَ نَنْهَاهُنَّ فَلاَ يَقْبَلْنَ قَالَ "إِذَا أَمَرْتُمُوهُنَّ وَ نَهَيْتُمُوهُنَّ فَقَدْ قَضَيْتُمْ مَا عَلَيْكُمْ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4534 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) who said:
"Inspire them with the love of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) and leave them in their natural simplicity."
records.arabicText: 4534 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "أَلْهِمُوهُنَّ حُبَّ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ ذَرُوهُنَّ بَلْهَاءَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4534 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Inspire them with the love of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) and leave them in their natural simplicity."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4535 - Ismail ibn Abi Ziyad narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his forefathers (peace be upon them), who said: The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
"Do not house your women in upper chambers, do not teach them writing, and do not teach them Surah Yusuf. Instead, teach them spinning and Surah An-Nur."
records.arabicText:
4535 - وَ رَوَى إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ أَبِي زِيَادٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ :
"لاَ تُنْزِلُوا نِسَاءَكُمُ اَلْغُرَفَ وَ لاَ تُعَلِّمُوهُنَّ اَلْكِتَابَةَ وَ لاَ تُعَلِّمُوهُنَّ سُورَةَ يُوسُفَ وَ عَلِّمُوهُنَّ اَلْمِغْزَلَ وَ سُورَةَ اَلنُّورِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4535 - Ismail ibn Abi Ziyad narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his forefathers (peace be upon them), who said: The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not house your women in upper chambers, do not teach them writing, and do not teach them Surah Yusuf. Instead, teach them spinning and Surah An-Nur."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4536 - Durays al-Kunasi narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) who said:
A woman came to the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) for some need, and he said to her: "Perhaps you are one of those who delay (their husbands)?"
She asked: "And who are those who delay, O Messenger of Allah (swt)?"
He (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "A woman whose husband calls her for some need, but she keeps delaying him until he becomes drowsy and falls asleep. Such a woman is continuously cursed by the angels until her husband wakes up."
records.arabicText:
4536 - وَ رَوَى ضُرَيْسٌ اَلْكُنَاسِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ:
"إِنَّ اِمْرَأَةً أَتَتْ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ لِبَعْضِ اَلْحَاجَةِ فَقَالَ لَهَا "لَعَلَّكِ مِنَ اَلْمُسَوِّفَاتِ" فَقَالَتْ وَ مَا اَلْمُسَوِّفَاتُ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ فَقَالَ "اَلْمَرْأَةُ يَدْعُوهَا زَوْجُهَا لِبَعْضِ اَلْحَاجَةِ فَلاَ تَزَالُ تُسَوِّفُهُ حَتَّى يَنْعُسَ زَوْجُهَا فَيَنَامَ فَتِلْكَ لاَ تَزَالُ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةُ تَلْعَنُهَا حَتَّى يَسْتَيْقِظَ زَوْجُهَا " ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4536 - Durays al-Kunasi narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
A woman came to the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) for some need, and he said to her: "Perhaps you are one of those who delay (their husbands)?"
She asked: "And who are those who delay, O Messenger of Allah (swt)?"
He (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "A woman whose husband calls her for some need, but she keeps delaying him until he becomes drowsy and falls asleep. Such a woman is continuously cursed by the angels until her husband wakes up."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4537 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"May Allah (swt) have mercy on a servant who treats his wife well, for Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has given him authority over her and made him her guardian."
records.arabicText: 4537 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "رَحِمَ اَللَّهُ عَبْداً أَحْسَنَ فِيمَا بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ زَوْجَتِهِ، فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ قَدْ مَلَّكَهُ نَاصِيَتَهَا وَ جَعَلَهُ اَلْقَيِّمَ عَلَيْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4537 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "May Allah (swt) have mercy on a servant who treats his wife well, for Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, has given him authority over her and made him her guardian."

records.chapter: Chapter on the wife's Rights Over Her Husband
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4538 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "The best of you is the one who is best to his wives, and I am the best of you to my wives."
records.arabicText: 4538 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "خَيْرُكُمْ خَيْرُكُمْ لِنِسَائِهِ وَ أَنَا خَيْرُكُمْ لِنِسَائِي ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4538 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The best of you is the one who is best to his wives, and I am the best of you to my wives."

records.chapter: Chapter on Coitus Interruptus (ʿazl)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4539 - Al-Qasim ibn Yahya narrated from his grandfather Al-Hasan ibn Rashid, from Yaqub al-Jufi, who said: I heard Abu al-Hasan (as) say:
"There is no harm in practicing withdrawal (coitus interruptus) in six cases; the woman who is certain that she cannot conceive, the elderly woman, the ill-mannered woman, the obscene woman, the woman who does not nurse her child, and the bondwoman."
records.arabicText: 4539 - رَوَى اَلْقَاسِمُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ جَدِّهِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ رَاشِدٍ عَنْ يَعْقُوبَ اَلْجُعْفِيِّ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ: "لاَ بَأْسَ بِالْعَزْلِ فِي سِتَّةِ وُجُوهٍ اَلْمَرْأَةِ اَلَّتِي أَيْقَنَتْ أَنَّهَا لاَ تَلِدُ وَ اَلْمُسِنَّةِ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ اَلسَّلِيطَةِ وَ اَلْبَذِيَّةِ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ اَلَّتِي لاَ تُرْضِعُ وَلَدَهَا وَ اَلْأَمَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4539 - Al-Qasim ibn Yahya narrated from his grandfather Al-Hasan ibn Rashid, from Yaqub al-Jufi, who said: I heard Abu al-Hasan (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in practicing withdrawal (coitus interruptus) in six cases; the woman who is certain that she cannot conceive, the elderly woman, the ill-mannered woman, the obscene woman, the woman who does not nurse her child, and the bondwoman."

records.chapter: Chapter on Protective Jealousy
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4540 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "My father Ibrahim (as) was protective, and I am more protective than him. May Allah (swt) humble the one among the believers who is not protective."
records.arabicText:
4540 - قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ :
"كَانَ أَبِي إِبْرَاهِيمُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ غَيُوراً وَ أَنَا أَغْيَرُ مِنْهُ وَ أَرْغَمَ اَللَّهُ أَنْفَ مَنْ لاَ يَغَارُ مِنَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4540 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "My father Ibrahim (as) was protective, and I am more protective than him. May Allah (swt) humble the one among the believers who is not protective."
